assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All 4 slices will be recreated simultaneously — not rolling upgrades' and 'Each 256-chip slice is treated as a single atomic unit that gets recreated entirely'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Cascading restart: A maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices in your multislice environment'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'All pods on all slices restart simultaneously when maintenance begins' and 'Your training job will be completely terminated across all 1,024 chips at once'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response addresses this with 'Capacity dependency: If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available' and mentions 1-3 hours downtime in worst case

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response has a dedicated section 'Save training checkpoint immediately' with specific kubectl commands to trigger manual checkpoints

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes checkpoint resume guidance with 'Restart training from latest checkpoint' section and 'Verify training resumed from checkpoint (not epoch 0)' commands

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or how to defer maintenance events - it only discusses preparing for and handling the maintenance

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Don't schedule maintenance during critical training phases' and 'TPU multislice maintenance should happen during planned training gaps' and 'Consider pausing training campaigns entirely during maintenance periods'
